assertions.reason: `audio_transcriptions_contract` passed `transcription_dispatches_typed_formats_and_preserves_multipart_semantics`, which proves multipart file fidelity, model/optional field forwarding, known-speaker inputs, and return-type dispatch for `json`, `verbose_json`, and `vtt`. `audio_speaker_metadata_contract` also proves `diarized_json` typed preservation. However, the observed mocked cargo surface does not exercise plain `text` or `srt` transcription outputs, so the full contract is not completely proven.

`audio_transcriptions_contract` passed `streaming_transcription_assembles_deltas_segments_and_usage` plus both truncated-stream negative tests. The source assertions confirm ordered `transcript.text.delta`, `transcript.text.segment`, and terminal `transcript.text.done` handling, final text/usage assembly, clean stream closure, and deterministic failure when the terminal done event is missing.

`audio_translations_contract` passed `translation_preserves_file_semantics_and_typed_bodies`, which proves multipart file fidelity, `prompt`/`temperature` forwarding, and response dispatch for structured `{ text }`, `verbose_json`, and `srt`. It also passed `translation_rejects_diarized_json_before_hitting_the_wire`. However, the observed mocked cargo surface does not exercise plain `text` or `vtt` translation outputs, so the full contract is not completely proven.

`audio_speech_contract` passed `speech_preserves_binary_and_non_json_stream_modes`, which asserts `Accept: application/octet-stream`, preserves `response_format`, `voice`, `speed`, `instructions`, and `stream_format`, returns raw bytes for binary/audio responses, and leaves SSE speech payloads as raw event-stream bytes without JSON coercion.

`audio_speaker_metadata_contract` passed `diarized_transcription_and_streaming_segments_preserve_speaker_metadata`, proving typed `diarized_json` responses retain speaker labels/turn segmentation and streaming `transcript.text.segment` events preserve per-speaker attribution. The same test also includes known-speaker inputs on the typed request path.

commands.observation: The `audio_transcriptions_contract` binary finished successfully with 4/4 tests passing: `transcription_dispatches_typed_formats_and_preserves_multipart_semantics`, `streaming_transcription_assembles_deltas_segments_and_usage`, and two truncated-stream negative tests.

The `audio_translations_contract` binary finished successfully with 2/2 tests passing: `translation_preserves_file_semantics_and_typed_bodies` and `translation_rejects_diarized_json_before_hitting_the_wire`.
The `audio_speech_contract` binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `speech_preserves_binary_and_non_json_stream_modes`.
The `audio_speaker_metadata_contract` binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `diarized_transcription_and_streaming_segments_preserve_speaker_metadata`.

frictions.description: Cargo output shows which test functions passed, but mapping those passes to the contract assertions required source inspection because the format-dispatch details are encoded inside the test bodies rather than emitted in test stdout.
frictions.resolution: Reviewed the four assigned Rust test files and used their assertions to determine which contract clauses were fully covered versus still unexercised.

blockers.description: The observed mocked cargo surface does not include transcription coverage for plain `text` or `srt` outputs, so VAL-MEDIA-004 cannot be fully proven from the assigned commands.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Inspected all assigned test binaries and assertion bodies; no additional transcription format fixture covering plain `text` or `srt` was present in the assigned surface.

blockers.description: The observed mocked cargo surface does not include translation coverage for plain `text` or `vtt` outputs, so VAL-MEDIA-006 cannot be fully proven from the assigned commands.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Inspected the assigned translation test binary and assertion body; no additional translation fixture covering plain `text` or `vtt` was present in the assigned surface.

summary: Ran all 4 assigned mocked cargo commands successfully. Confirmed 3 assertions passed (VAL-MEDIA-005, VAL-MEDIA-007, VAL-MEDIA-010) and marked 2 assertions blocked (VAL-MEDIA-004, VAL-MEDIA-006) because the assigned mocked cargo surface does not exercise every required output format.
